body: **Breaking:** `Checkbox.Root`, `Radio.Root` and `Switch.Root` renders `` instead of `

| --- _To edit notification comments on pull requests, go to your [Netlify project configuration](https://app.netlify.com/projects/base-ui/configuration/notifications#deploy-notifications)._

> Clicking the label to trigger the radio doesn't work if it's a span, but this can be added back via FieldItem

It "works" for me in the example, but the focus state doesn't sync properly. `onFocus()` on the hidden input to focus the `<span>` seems like it'd be a workaround (similar to `<Select.Trigger>` behavior)
This is still invalid nesting of interactive elements: ``` ``` Need to try to get `input type="hidden"` to work

On https://deploy-preview-3205--base-ui.netlify.app/experiments/forms/button-controls,
Clicking the label with the mouse doesn't set focus on the radio `<span>` control, so using <kbd>ArrowUp</kbd>/<kbd>ArrowDown</kbd> doesn't work. (There's also a mismatch in the focus style when tabbing into it and using the controls)
There's also an issue where the `label`'s `for` must match the `input`'s `id` instead of the `id` of the root element, in which case it should be labelled with `aria-labelledby="(label id)"`

@mj12albert yeah, `Select` forwards the `id` to the hidden input. Clicking the label focuses the hidden input, so `onFocus` is used to immediately relocate it to the trigger. I think that's the correct solution when using non-`<button>` triggers (although Select was recently changed to be a native `<button>`)

> `Select` forwards the `id` to the hidden input.

OK doign the same here but a gotcha with this is that `<Select.Trigger id="MyTrigger" />` id's the input and not the actual trigger, though I suppose manual ids are an edge case in 2025...

> > Clicking a plain sibling  (even with for/id set correctly) is unable to toggle span role="checkbox"
> 
> It seems to work with a minimal repro:

Oh yeah it works, I didn't update this comment yet 😅 
Originally I thought it wasn't working because there is a weird behavior where an identical test for Switch fails for Checkbox because it's flaky when using `user.click`

Features all seem intact, the only issue now is `` like in the demos messes up the accName: Manually setting `htmlFor`/`id` doesn't help because the `id` is forwarded to the `input`, so it doesn't link the span, only `aria-label` on the span works... what do you think? @atomiks

> in the demos messes up the accName:

Is this more so an axe false positive? Without `aria-label`, the enclosing label announces its text when focus is on the `<span>` checkbox. If so, I think it's okay since there are workarounds to satisfy it if necessary
Alright, with VO the differences seem negligible anyway

I have updated my project to use beta 5, but I am seeing a conflict because of this change. `CheckboxRootProps` was updated to extend `BaseUIComponentProps<'span', CheckboxRoot.State>,` but `CheckboxRoot` was not updated as well, so it still typed as `React.ForwardRefExoticComponent<CheckboxRootProps & React.RefAttributes<HTMLButtonElement>>`. This causes an issue because `CheckboxRoot` is expected to extend a button, but its props are expected to extend a span.

In my implementation, updating my custom props to extend either a button or a span both result in errors when passed into `CheckboxRoot`. I was using button previously, which now generates a "Types of property 'onCopy' are incompatible" error (I'm assuming because that's the first native prop that conflicts), but if I update to use span instead I get a "Types of property 'ref' are incompatible." error. This error completely breaks the current implementation, so the only work around I can think of for now would be to not extend a native HTML element in my props at all.
